metrics.narrative: The buyer has repeatedly named real operational pain but never put a number on it. Becca West said on 1 Apr 2026, 'the UI is probably the main struggle... the PO isn't tied to the invoice... the connection with NetSuite is clunky at best.' Vatricia added on 12 May 2026 that tail-spend leakage happens because 'someone has committed... and it's not shared, and so downstream we're seeing that invoice, we don't know who that is.' No cost, headcount, or cycle-time figure has been quantified on any call; the move is to build a savings model before the 16 Aug decision.

champion.narrative: Anita Newsom is the deal's origin: she previously implemented Zycus at Ventura Foods, made the ProcureCon introduction, and drove the 1 Apr 2026 intro call. What developed: a warm, credible internal referral with real product familiarity ('I really like the AI pieces of your platform'). What's missing: Anita has not appeared on the 12 May or 22 May 2026 calls, nor is she named in the 29 Jun RFP update — the process is now run entirely by Vatricia and Lisa with no visible champion push. Status: developing/cooling, at risk.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the only named competitor across all three matched calls — it is the incumbent up for renewal, not a new evaluation ('so our renewal is coming up, and so we're looking at do we wanna stay with our vendor,' Becca West, 1 Apr 2026). No alternative new vendor has been named on any call. The real competitive threat is inertia: renewing Coupa as-is or a no-decision outcome, especially with the economic buyer unengaged and no quantified business case. The wedge is Zycus's integrated platform (vs Coupa's disconnected PO/invoice flow) plus the Merlin/ANNA AI story, which tested well in the 22 May 2026 demo.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well documented and multi-sourced. Becca West (1 Apr 2026): Coupa's UI is disconnected and NetSuite integration is 'clunky at best.' Anita Newsom (1 Apr 2026): SOPs and specification documents live in a separate external platform from procurement. Vatricia (12 May 2026): the team is 'managing a lot in emails,' with tail-spend leakage because commitments go untracked downstream. The pain owner spans procurement (Anita, Vatricia) and finance systems (Becca).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec) have been discussed on any call, which is expected at Shortlisted. Becca West referenced 'our renewal is coming up' on 1 Apr 2026 as the forcing event but never gave a date, so the Coupa contract-expiration lever is unquantified. Visibility would come from asking directly for the Coupa renewal date to anchor urgency.

economic_buyer.narrative: No CFO, SVP Finance, or budget-holder has joined a single call or been named as an approver across all three matched touchpoints. Vatricia (a procurement lead who joined Manscaped ~1 month before the 12 May 2026 call) is running the RFP process and said 'decision would be... to be live 3 to 6 months,' but never named who signs off on a $130k spend. Eric Lam (SVP Finance & Accounting) exists as an account-level contact but has never appeared in this opp's evidence. The move is to name and reach the actual approver before 16 Aug.

decision_process.narrative: The process split in two: Lisa Miller ran an AP-focused RFP while Vatricia was given a separate procurement-only project on 12 May 2026 'in the event we don't find alignment between AP and procurement.' On 28 Apr 2026 Lisa confirmed Zycus would be included in the procurement RFP but excluded from the relevant stakeholder split was formalized on 29 Jun 2026 when Manscaped issued a dedicated Procurement-only RFP (responses due 16 Jul, decision due 16 Aug). No approval chain beyond Vatricia and Lisa has been named, and no senior/finance escalation has appeared on any call.

decision_criteria.narrative: Vatricia gave a detailed, confirmed criteria list on 12 May 2026: a centralized intake/supplier hub, document storage for NDAs and specs, NDA gates before RFP submission, bid-comparison reporting, PO tracking without full PO entry, and NetSuite plus LinkSquares integration. She also flagged AI interest: 'if that was a feature we would absolutely utilize it.' The 22 May 2026 demo (Srishti Paul) walked through eProcurement, supplier risk, and the ANNA negotiation agent directly against this list, with Teams integration and supplier risk visibility both called out as positives by the buyer.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of the vendors the buyer kept in its narrower, procurement-only RFP (formally re-issued 29 Jun 2026, response due 16 Jul 2026, decision due 16 Aug 2026), but the deal still has no engaged economic buyer after roughly four months, its champion has gone quiet since the 22 May 2026 demo, and no quantified business case has been built.

deal_risk.text: Only about 15 days sit between the buyer's stated decision date (16 Aug 2026) and the Salesforce close date (31 Aug 2026), leaving little room for contracting, legal, and signature if Zycus is selected.

The opportunity carries only two logged buyer-side Contact Roles (Lisa Miller, Becca West) despite Vatricia and Anita Newsom being the more active recent participants — a single-thread and data-hygiene risk.

win_position.text: Manscaped's own procurement lead relayed on 12 May 2026 that Zycus's platform 'tailored more' to procurement's needs than to AP's, and the buyer kept Zycus in its narrowed Procurement-only RFP issued 29 Jun 2026 rather than dropping it.

No economic buyer or executive sponsor has joined any of the three calls read (1 Apr, 12 May, 22 May 2026) or been named in any Next Step update; the decision currently rests with Vatricia, a procurement hire who joined Manscaped roughly a month before the 12 May 2026 call.

Champion Anita Newsom, whose prior Zycus experience drove the original introduction (1 Apr 2026 call), has not been evidenced on any call or Next Step entry since the 22 May 2026 demo — about seven weeks of silence as of this sweep.

Scope was narrowed from a full source-to-pay evaluation to procurement-only: Lisa's 28 Apr 2026 update confirmed Zycus would not be included in the AP RFP, and Manscaped formalized this on 29 Jun 2026 with a dedicated Procurement-only RFP.

deal_momentum.text: Manscaped issued a new, dated Procurement-only RFP on 29 Jun 2026 with concrete milestones: Zycus response due 16 Jul 2026 and buyer decision due 16 Aug 2026.
Manscaped replied by email on 16 Jun 2026 (the last independently verified two-way buyer touch) after Zycus shared updated, scope-limited pricing on 9 Jun 2026.

No demo, call, or stakeholder outreach has occurred since the 22 May 2026 full-platform demo; Vatricia told Zycus on 2 Jun 2026 that no further demo was needed, closing the main live-engagement channel.

customer_commitment.text: Vatricia confirmed on 2 Jun 2026 that she had reviewed the shared materials and did not need a follow-up demo, indicating active internal evaluation rather than disengagement.
No commercial commitment (LOI, budget confirmation, or signed NDA) exists; the buyer's only confirmed forward step is its own RFP process.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus matches the buyer's stated criteria closely on integration and centralization, but has not yet proven the AP/invoice-adjacent value now that AP has been split out of scope.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are tagged on the opportunity, but the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all empty. The Merlin attachment is Zycus-driven, not a reflection of documented buyer commentary.
• The April 1 call (26 minutes) with Lisa Miller, Becca West and Anita Newsom produced action items strictly on Showpad creation, supplier-management module questions covering quality and spec docs, and demo recordings spanning intake-to-pay, supplier performance management, project management and sourcing. Merlin, agentic AI and autonomous negotiation were not raised by the buyer.
• The May 12 follow-up was a short 18-minute session and also produced no AI signal. The buyer's focus is on confirming module functionality, especially spec document storage, and consuming information via Showpad. With no buyer AI quotes and the commentary fields blank, this lands at Curious with low confidence.
